title: laurembergia zeylanica
intro: Laurembergia zeylanica, also known as Ceylon tea or Indian tea, is a shrub-like plant native to Sri Lanka, characterized by its small white flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Laurembergia zeylanica include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, and infusates, which can be consumed hot or cold to reap its benefits.
